sentences.text: Task 1. Listen to the text and for each question mark the correct answer A, B, C or D.
You now have 40 seconds to look through the task.
You will then hear the recording twice.
Task 2. This advert of our travel agency provides information about some of the best places to see in New York.
One of the most visited places in New York is Times Square.
Americans like to say that out of 60 million people visiting New York every year, 50 million come and see Times Square.
Times Square, one of the busiest pedestrian areas in the world, is located in the heart of Manhattan, the cultural and business center of New York.
It is home to the city's entertainment industry with its popular Broadway theater district.
But Times Square is mostly famous for its enormous electrified adverts creating a surreal, mystical atmosphere.
Every night at exactly 11.57, the huge screens of Times Square transform into a three-minute art show, something really worth seeing.
So, New York is changed.
Times Square is truly the city that never sleeps.
Times Square got its current name in 1904 when the headquarters of a very popular newspaper, The Times, moved to the area.
This was the first time when the area became the epicenter of New York's social elite.
1904 was also the year when the first electrified adverts appeared in the square.
Times Square is also the place of the New Year's Eve ball drop.
It was one of the most famous New Year's Eve celebrations in the world.
In 1907, the first New Year's Eve ball drop was held at Times Square.
The tradition is still ongoing and nowadays more than 1 million people gather here on New Year's Eve to watch this beautiful event.
Also, it may be interesting to know that the largest crowd in its history, about 2 million people, celebrated the U.S. victory in World War II
on August 14, 1904.
The U.S. was the first city to celebrate the U.S. victory in World War II.
Another place worth seeing in New York is Central Park, one of the most famous parks in the world.
Central Park is the fifth largest park in New York City, covering more than 3,000 square kilometers.
It's the most visited park in the United States, with an estimated 42 million visitors annually.
Central Park spreads across more than 3,000 square meters and is home to many impressive buildings, bridges, and parks.
It's home to many amazing parks, skating rinks, sports fields, castles, a zoo, and much more.
The first areas in Central Park opened to the public in 1858.
Already in the 1890s, Central Park was a famous spot for ice skating.
While the park is located in Manhattan and is nowadays surrounded by tall buildings and city life,
it is hard to believe you are actually in a big city because it's so quiet and peaceful there.
According to one of the commercial websites offering virtual tours to New York, Central Park is the most filmed location in the world.
At least 352 movies have been shot in the park.
Did you know that some of the scenes from the popular film Home Alone 2 about a lost boy named Kevin were shot in Central Park?
The romantic Gap-style bridge and the popular skating rink of Central Park appear in several scenes of this film.
It's interesting to know that the profits from the top three most popular movies filmed in Central Park reached $200 million.
If you wish, a unique Central Park walking tour will take you to 40 locations that have been used in some of the most popular American TV shows and movies.
And don't forget to join our Facebook page and share your New York experience in the comments.
